Crossing_Comment: Overhead wires also visible.  Flowing downstream despite being after high tide. Second crossing at South Street about 50 feet downstream. Beyond South Street creek opens up to 50-100 feet wide then opens into bay. Veg US is comparable to veg below South St. Between culverts is almost all rip rap.  Good potential for salt marsh migration. DS crossing at South Street was recently replaced so this crossing is the last significant constriction limiting tidal exchange to upstream marsh. Extra images include one looking downstream from South Street toward bay.
Location_Description: At intersection with Riverside Ave and South Street
Structure_Comment: Metal invert deteriorated with many holes. Piping evident.  Headwall failing at inlet; see photos.

Crossing_Comment: Overhead lines also present. Bridge dated 1970. Wetland flagging observed at crossing. Strong current through crossing flowing ds at time of assessment made river right opening deeper-scour is occurring at this side and it is about 2 feet deeper on this side. Not a passage barrier but crossing should be significantly increased to match width of river.
Location_Description: Rte 6 bridge over the Lee River just east of Venus De Milo restaurant
Road: Rte 6 - GRAND ARMY OF THE REPUBLIC HIGHWAY

Crossing_Comment: Note that there is no second crossing at County Street as shown in the crossing map-- this crossing cuts under both streets at the intersection.  No downstream channel outlet is to a pool where water is impounded at low tide then the bay. Auto repair shop owner on corner indicated that it floods more frequently now after the high school was built. He said erosion is getting worse and trees are falling into the channel.  US channel has steep slope.  SMM potential seems low.  Should be flood tide based on time but flow is still in the downstream direction at time of assessment.  overhead wires and traffic lights at the crossing.
Location_Description: Intersection of Riverside Ave and County Street cutting diagonally under both roads.
Structure_Comment: rocks and sand berm at low tide about 50 feet below crossing. Structure is not aligned it bends 45 to 60 degrees inside to turn toward river right to reach the outlet. Appears to be 2 box culverts of slightly different sizes and construction. No floor on u

Crossing_Comment: Very difficult access due to vegetation. DS veg is a wall of phrag multiflora and poison ivy at road. US is a wall of phrag in the channel with multiflora and bittersweet at the road. There is a catch basin immediately over the crossing inlet on the road. See structure notes. The channel is a narrow confined and incised channel upstream with adjacent yards on both sides that are about 3 feet higher and likely built on fill.  the entire corridor between yards is only 8-10 feet with mowed lawns right up to edge of phrag. Tide does not seem to be overcoming the drop structure so no tidal range recorded upstream.
Location_Description: At guardrail and catch basin next to 47 Barneyville Road
Structure_Comment: granite mortar and brick headwall is rotating outward at outlet.  Inlet pipe is mostly submerged at low tide crosses road drops into catch basin then drops down to the outlet about 16" lower.  There is no aquatic passage at this structure and tide does not

Location_Description: Across from Marianna's Marina
Structure_Comment: Plan shows a 7.5 ft drop in elevation from inlet to outlet over 150 feet culvert

Crossing_Comment: Overhead wires also present. Crossing is 40 to 50 feet upstream of inlet to old RR crossing with hurricane barrier.  US veg is cattail knotweed freshwater veg; DS veg between RR and road crossing characterized by very steep slopes upland veg poison ivy; below RR crossing is salt marsh. Upstream of crossing channel was constructed and rerouted as part of a remediation response at adjacent brownfields-superfund site. Constructed check dams along the upstream reach have about a 1 foot stone drop.
Location_Description: guardrail at mile 10.791
Structure_Comment: utility pipe carried under bridge deck approx 5.5 feet above channel.

Crossing_Comment: DS veg is salt marsh and phragmites; us veg is freshwater; next upstream crossing is about 50 feet away at County Street; the crossing has an old hurricane barrier - there was a sluice gate that dropped down to block tides but the actual gate has been removed. Now there is a 2.6 foot drop down to the water surface which greatly restricts tidal influence. The next downstream crossing at Pleasant Street is in design for replacement and is already reasonably sized. This is the first major barrier to tidal flow from the Taunton into Muddy Cove Brook.
Location_Description: parallel to County Street crossing at mile 10.791 east of road approx 40 feet
